rulings.Case Title: JAMIE EDSON ET AL VS. AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF JAMIE EDSON INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC, CRIAG MULLINS TO LIFT STAY OF PROCEEDINGS is GRANTED. Stay is lifted. Non-opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA AVILES-LANDAU VS. VICTORIA MCBEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Ccp Sec 664.6 And Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT VICTORIA MCBEE, JOSHUA STEFFENS NOTICE OF MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO CCP SEC 664.6 AND ATTORNEY'S FEES is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show that it complied with the terms of the settlement agreement entitling them to the second installment payment "to be paid when keys are handed on May 15, 2024, provided compliance with agreement." The Court notes that this motion was filed on May 31, 2024, 6 days before Plaintiff filed a dismissal. In light of the dismissal and lack of any order retaining jurisdiction on file, the Court lacks jurisdiction to address any further issues related to the settlement agreement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BAYMARK FINANCIAL, INC. ET AL VS. SALLY L. LIU
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT SALLY LIU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Prior unlawful detainer based on the same operative notice was dismissed for failure to amend. Plaintiff was given an opportunity to file a supplemental brief to address this issue. Plaintiff filed a supplemental brief citing inapposite cases and not addressing the cases on point discussing the availability of voluntary dismissal following a failure to amend. E.g. Wells v. Marina City Properties, Inc. (1981) 29 Cal.3d 781: "once a general demurrer is sustained with leave to amend and plaintiff does not so amend within the time authorized by the court or otherwise extended by stipulation or appropriate order, he can no longer voluntarily dismiss his action pursuant to section 581, subdivision 1, even if the trial court has yet to enter a judgment of dismissal on the sustained demurrer." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HANCHEN ZHANG VS. EDWIN LAI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2024 line 11. DEFENDANT EDWIN LAI, MARIKA REBECCA LOVELACE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege whether TPA 2019, SFRO or neither apply to the tenancy and whether Plaintiff complied with applicable eviction protection scheme. Allegations must be made in good faith. If Plaintiff asserts that SFRO applies, Plaintiff must allege that form 1007 had been served on the Defendant per SFRO 37.9(c). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
